_comment: Named deployments. Each selects an operating mode (simulation, ingestion, scenario, physics), a security profile, the point model, the tag database/scenario/grid, and which bench simulators to start (sims). 'category' and 'use_when' group them in the control console so a user can find the right one quickly. northbound is always TASE.2/ICCP over MMS.

sim-demo.description: Simulation mode. The server drives every point with synthetic values (simulateValues); connects to nothing. For training, capture, and parser/IDS testing.
sim-demo.use_when: Fastest look at the HMI. Synthetic data, connects to nothing.

field-demo.description: Universal ingestion: one gateway ingests Modbus (plc1/plc2) and DNP3 (rtu1) at the same time, with plc3 unreachable to show a degraded station. Uses the bundled Modbus and DNP3 simulators so it runs with no hardware.
field-demo.use_when: The full real pipeline: Modbus and DNP3 ingested into ICCP.

physics-demo.description: Physics mode. A DC power-flow co-simulation (config/grid.json) drives the points from a real four-bus grid model. Flows are live; open the main tie breaker (plc1_brk) from the HMI or an attack and watch the parallel line overload and the failure cascade line by line. No gateway, no simulation.
physics-demo.use_when: A live power-flow grid. Open the main tie breaker to watch a cascade.

scenario-demo.description: Scenario mode. A deterministic, scripted timeline (scenarios/fdi_tieline.json) is the value source: normal operations plus a false-data injection, a spoofed alarm, an unauthorized breaker open, and a comms outage. No gateway, no simulation. Writes a ground-truth label timeline for datasets and detection scoring. Reproducible every run.
scenario-demo.use_when: A scripted, repeatable run of injection, spoof, unauthorized command, and comms loss.

cascade-demo.description: Scenario plus physics: a scripted attack on a live power-flow grid. An injection masks the tie-line flow, then an unauthorized breaker open triggers a real cascading blackout, all on the one grid model, with ground truth labelling the attack and recording the cascade. The force multiplier: scripted attacks with physical consequences, ready to capture, label, and score.
cascade-demo.use_when: A scripted attack on a live grid that triggers a real cascading blackout.

ukraine2015-attack.description: Reproduces the December 2015 Ukraine grid blackout (Sandworm / BlackEnergy3) over TASE.2: a second association reads the whole model, opens breakers across the bay, re-sends open commands to deny operators control, and takes a station's view offline, with a real cascade on the live grid. Writes a ground-truth label timeline. See scenarios/ukraine2015_blackout.json.
ukraine2015-attack.use_when: The 2015 Ukraine blackout: recon, coordinated breaker opens, operators locked out, cascade.

industroyer-attack.description: Reproduces the 2016 Industroyer / CRASHOVERRIDE substation attack pattern over TASE.2: automated discovery, a manipulated operator view, a coordinated breaker sweep, and a rapid open/close toggling flood, against the live grid. See scenarios/industroyer_sweep.json.
industroyer-attack.use_when: The 2016 Industroyer breaker sweep: automated discovery, sweep, and a toggling flood.

stealthy-attack.description: The quiet attack: no breaker open, just point-and-tag identification, a modified setpoint, and false telemetry that hides a developing problem (manipulation of view and alarm suppression). The hard-to-detect class. See scenarios/stealthy_false_data.json.
stealthy-attack.use_when: Stealthy false data: modify a setpoint, then mask the result. Hard to detect.

recon-attack.description: Reconnaissance and collection only: an attacker reads the model and the bilateral table, re-reads every point, and singles out the breakers, with no command sent. A clean detection target for catching the stage before impact. See scenarios/recon_collection.json.
recon-attack.use_when: Pure reconnaissance: model and point reads, no commands. Catch it before impact.

field-hardened.description: The universal multi-protocol VIRTUAL deployment, hardened: mutual TLS (Secure ICCP) plus a loopback command allowlist. Run scripts/gen_certs.sh first.
field-hardened.use_when: Mutual TLS and a command allowlist. Test your defenses against the attacks.

field-federated.description: Multi-control-centre federation: the universal ingestion stack with an ENFORCED bilateral table (config/blt.conf). Each peer is scoped by the table to the data it may read and the controls it may operate; reads, controls, and report members outside a peer's agreement are denied or withheld. The local components have full access on loopback; edit config/blt.conf to add partner control centres.
field-federated.use_when: An enforced bilateral table: per-peer read and control scoping.

federation-demo.description: Multi-control-centre federation: two TASE.2 servers (CC-A and CC-B), each its own control centre, with a live tie between them. The relay carries CC-A's tie-line data across the intertie into CC-B over real ICCP, so CC-B's HMI shows another utility's data it does not measure locally. What A shares is its bilateral agreement; set a bilateral table on A to enforce it. Open the HMI to watch CC-A's flow arrive at CC-B.
federation-demo.use_when: Two control centres with a live tie; watch CC-A's data arrive at CC-B.

physical.description: PHYSICAL testbed: real PLCs and RTUs, no simulators. Template to customize: edit config/scada.json for your points and create ingest/tags.json pointing at your devices. Set security to hardened for a real trust boundary. See docs guide 'Connecting physical PLCs and RTUs'.
physical.use_when: Real PLCs and RTUs. Edit the tag database to point at your devices.
